title: Types marked InitializeOnLoad require a static constructor
messageFormat: Add a static constructor to this type or remove the InitializeOnLoad attribute.
summary: The InitializeOnLoad attribute tells the editor to run a type's static initialisation as soon as the project loads. Unity triggers that initialisation by invoking the type's static constructor, so a class carrying the attribute with no static constructor declares an intent it cannot honour.

Either add the static constructor that holds the load-time setup, or drop the attribute if no editor-load behaviour is needed. Leaving the attribute without a static constructor is dead intent that misleads the next reader.
